text_original: Gravity is the root of lightness;
text_normalized: Gravity is the root of lightness;

text_original: Therefore a wise prince, marching the whole day, does not go far from his baggage waggons.
text_normalized: Therefore a wise prince, marching the whole day, does not go far from his baggage waggons.

text_original: Although he may have brilliant prospects to look at, he quietly remains (in his proper place), indifferent to them.
text_normalized: Although he may have brilliant prospects to look at, he quietly remains (in his proper place), indifferent to them.

text_original: How should the lord of a myriad chariots carry himself lightly before the kingdom?
text_normalized: How should the lord of a myriad chariots carry himself lightly before the kingdom?

text_original: If he do act lightly, he has lost his root (of gravity);
text_normalized: If he do act lightly, he has lost his root (of gravity);

text_original: if he proceed to active movement, he will lose his throne.
text_normalized: if he proceed to active movement, he will lose his throne.
